textPlain: Not a contribution but a question - how do one-person SaaS founders find customers in 2025?It feels like between most subreddits banning self promotion and LinkedIn being flooded by AI post and inboxes by AI cold calls, it should be harder to make yourself stand out to a stranger.Do you have be a content creator to grow an audience, make it work and get your first clients?
I run readlang.com as one person. I started it back in 2012 and it currently makes about 14K euros / month, with expenses of about 1.5K, so it's mostly profit.

Often solo-founders build very niche apps/SaaS which they already have a personal network for or can target smaller platforms and business networks for marketing/distribution. You can stay small and do very well.The larger platforms are usually too saturated to be effective. In my experience, generally not a good return on $/time.
